text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 56-year-old female with Stage IV BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic cutaneous melanoma, previously treated with Tafinlar/tram (progressive disease at approximately 8 months), subsequently initiated on Yervoy 1 mg/kg plus nivo 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks. Course complicated by grade 3 immune-mediated colitis during cycle 3, requiring hospitalization, permanent discontinuation of Yervoy, and high-dose corticosteroid therapy. Currently on oral prednisone taper. Presenting for response assessment and surveillance imaging. Reference studies: prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain.

TECHNIQUE:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: Helical acquisition was performed from the lung apices through the ischial tuberosities following administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast material, with images obtained in the portal venous phase. Multiplanar reformats were generated and reviewed. Slice thickness 3 mm.

MRI brain: Standard protocol with sagittal T1, axial T2/FLAIR, axial DWI/ADC, axial T2* GRE, and axial T1 post-gadolinium (Gadavist 0.1 mmol/kg IV) sequences obtained at 3.0 Tesla. No artifacts limiting diagnostic evaluation.

--- CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS ---

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK (visualized): Not included on this examination. MRI brain obtained separately (see below).

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are well aerated bilaterally. Previously identified pulmonary nodules in the right lower lobe have substantially decreased. A subpleural nodule in the right lower lobe previously measuring 0.6 cm now measures approximately 0.3 cm, and a second nodule previously measuring 0.4 cm is now either not confidently identified or measures no larger than 0.2 cm at its maximum dimension, falling below the threshold of reliable measurement on CT. No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. The major airways are patent. No endobronchial lesion is seen.

Mediastinum: The mediastinal contour is unremarkable. The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The aorta is normal in caliber without evidence of aneurysm or dissection.

Breast and Soft Tissue of Chest Wall: No suspicious chest wall mass or axillary adenopathy in the right axilla to suggest new disease. Previously enlarged right axillary lymph node now measures approximately 0.9 cm in short-axis diameter (previously 1.6 cm on most recent prior study). Left axillary lymph node cluster previously measuring 1.1 cm in short axis is now approximately 0.6 cm. These findings are in keeping with interval improvement in bilateral axillary adenopathy. No new supraclavicular or infraclavicular adenopathy.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and contour. Previously identified hepatic metastases have demonstrated meaningful interval decrease. The dominant lesion in the right hepatic lobe, previously measuring 2.3 cm in the portal venous phase, now measures approximately 1.4 cm. A second right lobe lesion, previously measuring 1.4 cm, has decreased to approximately 0.7 cm. A third small right lobe lesion previously described as a new finding measuring 0.8 cm on the prior study is no longer confidently identified on the current examination. No new hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein is patent.

Spleen: Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Visualized portions of the pancreas appear unremarkable. No focal pancreatic lesion or pancreatic ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands appear within normal limits. No adrenal nodule or mass is identified.

Kidneys and Ureters: The kidneys are symmetric in size and enhancement. No hydronephrosis, nephrolithiasis, or renal mass. The ureters are not dilated.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The stomach is unremarkable. Small bowel loops are normal in caliber and distribution. The colon appears mildly thickened in the rectosigmoid region and descending colon, consistent with the known history of recent immune-mediated colitis; this finding has improved compared to prior examination. No pneumoperitoneum. No bowel obstruction. No acute mesenteric finding.

Retroperitoneum / Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. No para-aortic, iliac, or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Abdominal Wall and Soft Tissues: No subcutaneous masses or nodules are identified in the abdomen. Previously noted subcutaneous nodularity in the right flank has not been visualized on this or prior restaging studies, consistent with prior complete response at those sites.

PELVIS:

Bladder: Unremarkable. No pelvic free fluid. Uterus and adnexa appear unremarkable for age. No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Bone and Soft Tissues: The visualized osseous structures are without suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion. No pathologic fracture or periosteal reaction identified. No soft tissue mass.

--- MRI BRAIN ---

FINDINGS:

No intra-axial or extra-axial enhancing lesions are identified to suggest intracranial metastatic disease. No leptomeningeal enhancement. The brain parenchyma demonstrates normal signal intensity on T1 and T2/FLAIR sequences. No evidence of hemorrhage on gradient echo sequences. No restricted diffusion to suggest acute infarction. The ventricles and sulci are appropriate for age. The cerebellum, brainstem, and posterior fossa structures are unremarkable. The pituitary gland is normal in size and signal; no pituitary enlargement or infundibular thickening to suggest hypophysitis (of note, patient is on active steroid therapy at this time). The sella is within normal limits. Orbits and visualized paranasal sinuses are unremarkable.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy has substantially decreased compared to the most recent prior restaging CT: right axillary lymph node now approximately 0.9 cm (previously 1.6 cm) and left axillary lymph node now approximately 0.6 cm (previously 1.1 cm), consistent with a meaningful treatment response.

2. Hepatic metastases have decreased in size: the dominant right lobe lesion has decreased from 2.3 cm to approximately 1.4 cm, and a second right lobe lesion has decreased from 1.4 cm to approximately 0.7 cm. A third small hepatic lesion previously identified on the prior study is no longer seen on the current examination.

3. Right lower lobe pulmonary nodules have substantially diminished; the larger of the two has decreased from 0.6 cm to 0.3 cm, and the smaller is no longer reliably identified.

4. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No new lymphadenopathy, pulmonary nodules, subcutaneous deposits, or osseous lesions.

5. Mild persistent wall thickening of the descending colon and rectosigmoid, improved compared with prior, consistent with the patient's known history of recent immune-mediated colitis in the setting of checkpoint inhibitor therapy.

6. MRI brain: No evidence of intracranial metastatic disease. Normal pituitary gland without features of hypophysitis.

Overall, these findings are consistent with a meaningful partial response to prior ipi/Opdivo combination immunotherapy across all previously identified sites of disease, including the liver, axillary nodal stations, and lungs. The absence of any new lesion is reassuring.

Attending Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
Radiology Fellow: [Electronically signed]
Dictated and finalized.
